theoremName: Xi augmented trace limit-identification consequence theorem
statement: Strong graph-dual convergence of the finite augmented traces R_aug,N to the closed trace R_aug identifies the augmented trace coordinate of weak graph limits in the liminf representative compactness argument.

closedAugmentedTraceConvergenceInputStat.label: closed augmented trace convergence input
closedAugmentedTraceConvergenceInputStat.reason: Imported from the Xi augmented trace convergence theorem: R_aug,N converges to the closed augmented trace R_aug in the transported graph-dual topology.

augmentedTraceGraphConvergenceInputStatu.label: augmented trace graph convergence input
augmentedTraceGraphConvergenceInputStatu.reason: The same theorem exports convergence in the completed augmented trace graph topology.

traceLimitIdentificationStatus.label: trace limit identification
traceLimitIdentificationStatus.reason: For bounded representative sequences with weak graph limits, the strong graph-dual trace convergence identifies the limiting augmented trace coordinate.

proof: Apply these convergences to the weak graph limit supplied by the liminf representative compactness argument.
Expose only trace-limit identification to downstream quotient-liminf code.
